citation.anchor: PAL_PERF_MON_INFO is called to determine the number of performance monitors and the events which can be counted

preconditions: The PAL firmware gate and a writable 128-byte aligned guest buffer are present
The entire valid buffer and a separate invalid-call sentinel begin with test-owned poison values
inputs: Test-owned PMC, PMD, cycle-event, and retired-bundle masks
expected_results: The valid call returns status zero and pm_info 0x3c04
The PMC mask is exactly 0xff and the PMD mask exactly 0xf0
All cycle and retired-bundle capability mask words are zero
Null and misaligned calls return -2 with zero reserved return payloads
allowed_results: No event-selection encoding is advertised because PMU event counting is outside this checkpoint
cases.committed_prefix: The valid call writes one complete four-mask result; invalid calls do not expose a partial buffer result
cases.restart_action: Each call returns directly to its declared continuation without architectural retry

oracle.derivation: The packed pm_info value and every mask word are test-owned literals derived from the cited PAL ABI and the declared four-pair, 60-bit product profile; no expectation is read from firmware or CPU implementation state

limitations: Event selection, counting, overflow, freeze, and interrupt delivery remain unimplemented and unclaimed

citation.anchor: Reading of an unimplemented PMC or PMD register returns zero

preconditions: Every write source is all one bits with NaT clear
The loop traverses every eight-bit selector exactly once
expected_results: The mismatch count is zero and terminal selector count is 256
allowed_results: No event-counting state transition is inferred from configuration-register storage
permitted_state_changes: Writable fields of PMC0 and PMC4 through PMC7
required_unchanged_state: Guest code and unrelated architectural registers
cases.committed_prefix: Every selector write is serialized and compared before the next selector is visited
cases.restart_action: No access faults or restarts at CPL0

oracle.derivation: The four selector classes and exact 0xf1, zero, 0xff7f, and zero results are encoded as test-owned constants derived from the cited register layouts and declared product profile

limitations: Lower-privilege fault priority and performance-event behavior are separate closure units

citation.anchor: Reading of an unimplemented PMC or PMD register returns zero

preconditions: Every write source is all one bits with NaT clear
The loop traverses every eight-bit selector exactly once
expected_results: The mismatch count is zero and terminal selector count is 256
allowed_results: No increment is expected because event counting is not advertised or stimulated
permitted_state_changes: The low 60 bits of PMD4 through PMD7
required_unchanged_state: Guest code and unrelated architectural registers
cases.committed_prefix: Every selector write is serialized and compared before the next selector is visited
cases.restart_action: No access faults or restarts at CPL0

oracle.derivation: The selector boundaries and 60-bit expected value are test-owned constants derived from the cited generic-counter width rule and declared product profile

limitations: Lower-privilege read controls and performance-event behavior are separate closure units
